MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-02-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ROD GOYA, and, CARMEL MISSION PARTNERSHIP MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: Kathleen P. Clack, Esq. Attorney at Law, (559) 241-7229 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Joshua N. Rosen, Esq. of Bledsore, Cathcart, Diestel, pedersen & Treppa LLP (415) 981-5411, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Triable issue of fact whether the statute of limitations should run from 2006 when plaintiff discovered from Dr. Marinkovich that her illness was due to mold. Triable issue of fact raised whether the 2006 lease containing the waiver provision (paragraph 8(c)) would bar any claim. Triable issue of fact regarding which lease would control over the various causes of action. Triable issue of fact raised whether plaintiff was commercially frustrated from using the unit she leased for the specific purpose of running her office. Triable issue of fact whether paragraph 6(b) of the 2006 lease would apply to bar the claim for commercial frustration in light of paragraphs 6(a), 7(b), and 7(c) which appear to not bar claims where defendant is at fault. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
